evidence: User asked only to reformat a transcript verbatim.
consequence: Storing it creates a fictitious 'transcript formatting' project tied to third-party app content.
Mislabeling loses a real academic deliverable OR over-elevates a tool-learning chat.

evidence: Lecture study, OS lab, Amazon resume, YouTube policy, linear algebra are unrelated.
consequence: Over-merge fuses unrelated activities into one fictitious project.

evidence: Codex titles e.g. 'are my assistant for final project I am doing'.
consequence: Copied titles violate the canonical-title rule and degrade downstream readability.

evidence: Codex knowledge descriptions are truncated raw pasted text, sometimes assistant/tool narration mislabeled as user tasks.
consequence: Generic knowledge is not reconstructable and can misattribute assistant/tool content to the user.
